name: The Lane Law Firm - San Antonio TX
description_short: Texas bankruptcy and business debt relief law firm specializing in personal/business bankruptcy, merchant cash advance defense, and denied insurance claims.
description_long:
The Lane Law Firm is a Texas-based legal practice headquartered in San Antonio with additional offices in Houston, Dallas, Austin, and New York. Founded on the principle of specialization, the firm explicitly focuses on two core practice areas—small business bankruptcy and denied/undervalued insurance claims—rather than offering broad legal services. The firm positions itself as "the market leader" in these niches through what they describe as "hard work, focus and outstanding results."

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy and debt relief services including personal bankruptcy (Chapter 7/13), business bankruptcy reorganization, merchant cash advance loan defense, SBA loan restructuring, and business debt collection defense. They also handle property insurance claims, including denied homeowner and commercial coverage disputes. The firm operates on a contingency model for insurance claims ("if we don't win... you don't pay"), reducing financial risk for claimants. For business debt, they advertise alternative strategies beyond consolidation or additional borrowing.

The Lane Law Firm differentiates itself through a stated commitment to specialization—they explicitly note "we don't do it all, but we are the best at what we do." Client reviews highlight responsive communication, personalized solutions, integrity, and attorneys like Steven and Frances Melendez who provide proactive follow-up. The firm targets specific professional collaborations (CPAs, contractors, roofers) suggesting industry expertise. They offer free case evaluations and same-day consultation availability.

The firm's primary limitation is geographic scope—while they serve Texas and New York, they are not a national provider. The website provides limited detail on specific outcomes, case success rates, or attorney credentials beyond testimonials. For consumers outside their service areas or seeking non-bankruptcy debt solutions, this firm would not be suitable. Potential clients should verify they handle the specific type of bankruptcy or insurance claim relevant to their situation before consultation.
address: 1100 Northwest Loop 410 Suite 700, San Antonio, TX 78213

company_info.headquarters: 1100 Northwest Loop 410 Suite 700, San Antonio, TX 78213

pros: Contingency-based representation for property insurance claims—no payment required if claim is unsuccessful
Free case evaluations and same-day consultation availability
Specialization focus: explicitly limits practice to bankruptcy and insurance to ensure expertise rather than generalist approach
Multiple office locations across Texas (Houston, Dallas, San Antonio, Austin) plus New York for geographic accessibility
Proactive communication and follow-up noted in client reviews; specific attorney names (Steven, Frances Melendez) mentioned for consistent service
Targeted expertise in predatory lending defense, merchant cash advances, and SBA loan restructuring—specialized knowledge areas
Professional collaboration network with CPAs, accountants, and contractors suggesting integrated business debt solutions
cons: Limited geographic service area—primarily Texas and New York; not a national provider for out-of-state bankruptcy filers
Website provides no specific case outcomes, success rates, bankruptcy discharge rates, or quantified performance metrics
No attorney credential details, years of experience, or board certifications listed on website; information limited to client testimonials
Unclear fee structure for bankruptcy cases—only insurance claims explicitly stated as contingency; personal/business bankruptcy fees not transparent
No information on average timeline for bankruptcy filing, discharge, or claim resolution
best_for: Texas small business owners with merchant cash advance debt, predatory SBA loans, or debt collector harassment seeking alternatives to consolidation
Texas homeowners or property owners with denied or undervalued property insurance claims who want no-upfront-cost representation
Business owners facing business bankruptcy reorganization (Chapter 11) or liquidation (Chapter 7) who need Texas-based counsel
Individuals in Texas with high personal debt considering Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy protection
diagnosis: The Lane Law Firm is best for Texas-based individuals and small business owners facing bankruptcy, merchant cash advance predatory lending, or denied insurance claims who value specialization and responsive attorney communication. The critical caveat is geographic limitation—the firm primarily serves Texas; out-of-state filers or claimants should verify coverage before engaging, and bankruptcy fee transparency is absent from the website.
services: Personal bankruptcy filing and representation (Chapter 7 and Chapter 13)
Merchant cash advance loan defense and restructuring
SBA loan debt relief and restructuring
Business debt collector defense and illegal collection practice counterclaims
Business debt consolidation alternatives and negotiated reduction strategies
Denied property insurance claim appeals and litigation
Undervalued property insurance claim negotiation and litigation
Free legal consultations and case evaluations
Collaboration support for CPAs, accountants, and contractors facing business insolvency
